vitals.terrain: Classic routes through forest roads and onsen towns

course_description.overview: The official Japanese edition of L'Eroica and the only Eroica event in Asia, featuring vintage bikes on classic routes through forest roads and onsen towns with significant climbing.

key_segments.name: Main Route
key_segments.description: Full distance with 3,855 m elevation gain on paved classic roads.

key_segments.name: Medium Route
key_segments.description: Moderate distance with 2,997 m elevation gain.

key_segments.name: Short Route
key_segments.description: Shorter option with 1,499 m elevation gain.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Challenging with 3,855 m of climbing over 182 km on vintage bikes, emphasizing retro cycling experience on demanding forested and mountainous terrain.

logistics.getting_there: Fly into Tokyo (Narita or Haneda airports), then take train or bus to Kitakaruizawa in Gunma Prefecture.
logistics.accommodation: Onsen towns nearby offer traditional ryokans and hotels; book early for event weekend.
logistics.registration_details: Registration via official Eroica website; limited to vintage bikes; check for 2026 openings.
logistics.bike_logistics: Vintage bikes required (pre-1987); transport via Japan rail or fly with bike; local bike shops in Karuizawa area.
logistics.local_considerations: Spring weather in April can be cool with possible rain; onsen culture for recovery; respect vintage bike rules.

final_verdict.one_liner: Iconic vintage road event blending Japanese onsen culture with Eroica's retro prestige.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Vintage bike enthusiasts seeking a challenging, cultural road cycling experience in Asia's only Eroica.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for the unique combination of epic climbing, history, and immersive Japanese scenery.
pro_tips: Use a compliant vintage bike; train for 3,800m+ climbing.
Stay in an onsen ryokan for post-ride recovery.
Register early as spots fill fast.
Pack for variable April mountain weather.
